channel.title: Publications — Bobby Zhao Sheng Lo, MD, PhD

item.title: Changes in nasal and throat microbiota composition during and after mupirocin and chlorhexidine decolonization treatment in asymptomatic methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus carriers: a longitudinal observational study
item.description: Testing a standard MRSA decolonization treatment showed that while it successfully cleared the bacteria in most carriers, it also caused long-lasting disruptions to their nasal microbiomes.

item.title: Forecasting the Incidence and Prevalence of Inflammatory Bowel Disease Across Nine Epidemiologic Stage 3 Regions
item.description: Forecasting the Incidence and Prevalence of Inflammatory Bowel Disease Across Nine Epidemiologic Stage 3 Regions

item.title: AI-BUS: AI Bowel Ultrasound System
item.description: AI-BUS: AI Bowel Ultrasound System

item.title: Clinical Trial: Evaluating a Single 1600 mg Tablet Regimen of 5‐Aminosalicylate for Ulcerative Colitis—The <scp>EASI</scp> Trial
item.description: A clinical trial comparing a single daily tablet for ulcerative colitis to three tablets found that both treatments resulted in similar patient adherence and disease relapse rates.

item.title: Incidence and Long‐Term Outcomes of Pouchitis in Ulcerative Colitis Patients
item.description: A study of ulcerative colitis patients after pouch surgery found that half experienced pouchitis, but urgent surgery did not increase this risk, and overall pouch failure was low.

item.title: The impact of the coronavirus (COVID-19) pandemic on individuals with gastrointestinal disorders: A protocol of an international collaborative study
item.description: The impact of the coronavirus (COVID-19) pandemic on individuals with gastrointestinal disorders: A protocol of an international collaborative study

item.title: 49: THE INCIDENCE AND PREVALENCE OF INFLAMMATORY BOWEL DISEASE IN NINE NATIONS ACROSS THE GLOBE: AN ANALYSIS OF THE 3RD EPIDEMIOLOGIC STAGE.
item.description: 49: THE INCIDENCE AND PREVALENCE OF INFLAMMATORY BOWEL DISEASE IN NINE NATIONS ACROSS THE GLOBE: AN ANALYSIS OF THE 3RD EPIDEMIOLOGIC STAGE.

item.title: Global evolution of inflammatory bowel disease across epidemiologic stages
item.description: By analyzing a century of data from 522 global studies, researchers mapped the real-world progression of inflammatory bowel disease, confirming its transition through four previously theoretical epidemiological stages.

item.title: Improving the Real-Time Classification of Disease Severity in Ulcerative Colitis: Artificial Intelligence as the Trigger for a Second Opinion
item.description: An AI system tested for classifying ulcerative colitis severity from endoscopy videos matched expert accuracy and improved the diagnostic performance of non-specialist doctors.

item.title: P0290 Predicting disease remission in Ulcerative Colitis patients - early results from the PRESAGER study
item.description: This study analyzed predictors of ulcerative colitis remission, finding that older age and higher disability reduced its likelihood, while longer disease duration and moderate activity increased it.

item.title: Clinical Outcomes After First-Line Anti- Tumor-Necrosis-Factor Treatment of Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease—A Prospective Multicenter Cohort Study
item.description: Testing anti-TNF therapy in patients with inflammatory bowel disease showed that while most responded initially, about a quarter to a third eventually lost response during maintenance therapy.

item.title: Histological, But Neither Clinical Nor Endoscopic Activity Predicts the Risk of Colectomy in Patients With Ulcerative Colitis Treated With Biologics
item.description: A study of ulcerative colitis patients starting biological therapy found that severe baseline tissue inflammation, but not endoscopic activity, independently predicts a higher risk of colon removal.

item.title: Probiotic Treatment of Ulcerative Colitis with Trichuris Suis Ova: A Randomised, Double-blinded, Placebo-controlled Clinical Trial [the PROCTO Trial]
item.description: A clinical trial testing Trichuris suis ova for moderate ulcerative colitis found that the treatment was not superior to placebo in achieving clinical remission at 24 weeks.

item.title: Building an AI Support Tool for Real-Time Ulcerative Colitis Diagnosis
item.description: Building an AI Support Tool for Real-Time Ulcerative Colitis Diagnosis

item.title: Specific Antibiotics Increase the Risk of Flare-Ups in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Results from a Danish Nationwide Population-Based Nested Case-Control Study
item.description: A study of Danish patients found that using specific classes of antibiotics is associated with a significantly increased risk of inflammatory bowel disease flare-ups.

item.title: The Global Evolution of Inflammatory Bowel Disease across Four Epidemiologic Stages
item.description: Analyzing a century of global data, researchers mapped how inflammatory bowel disease transitions through distinct epidemiological stages, confirming its progression from low incidence to compounding prevalence.

item.title: P1240 Systematic review with meta-analysis: faecal microbiota transplantation as treatment of chronic pouchitis
item.description: A systematic review found that fecal microbiota transplantation was no more effective than a placebo at improving short-term symptoms in patients with chronic pouchitis.

item.title: P375 Histological activity predicts the risk of colectomy after biological treatment in biological-naive patients with Ulcerative Colitis
item.description: Testing ulcerative colitis patients starting biological therapy, researchers found that baseline gut tissue inflammation significantly predicted the risk of colon removal, while clinical and endoscopic symptoms did not.

item.title: P1174 The disease course of elderly patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease two years prior to their death
item.description: By examining elderly inflammatory bowel disease patients before death, researchers found that hospitalizations, corticosteroid prescriptions, and disease relapses spiked during the final two months of life.

item.title: P598 Validating and Enhancing Real-time Disease Severity Classification in Ulcerative Colitis: Artificial Intelligence as a Second Opinion Trigger
item.description: An AI model tested for evaluating ulcerative colitis severity from endoscopy videos matched expert accuracy and improved the diagnostic performance of non-specialist doctors.

item.title: Fecal microbiota transplantation for the treatment of irritable bowel syndrome: A systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A review of fecal microbiota transplantation for irritable bowel syndrome found insufficient evidence to support its use, as it did not significantly improve symptoms compared to a placebo.

item.title: Tu1715 A RANDOMIZED, DOUBLE-BLIND, PLACEBO-CONTROLLED CLINICAL TRIAL ON EFFICACY AND SAFETY OF PROBIOTIC TREATMENT WITH TRICHURIS SUIS OVA IN PATIENTS WITH ULCERATIVE COLITIS
item.description: Tu1715 A RANDOMIZED, DOUBLE-BLIND, PLACEBO-CONTROLLED CLINICAL TRIAL ON EFFICACY AND SAFETY OF PROBIOTIC TREATMENT WITH TRICHURIS SUIS OVA IN PATIENTS WITH ULCERATIVE COLITIS

item.title: Identifying patients with inflammatory bowel disease in the Danish National Patient Register.
item.description: Researchers tested a new algorithm for identifying inflammatory bowel disease patients in Denmark's national registry, finding it is more accurate than the currently used method.

item.title: Exploring the Impact of Covid-19-Related Perceptions on Psychological Distress and Quality of Life in an International Gastrointestinal Cohort Over Time Guided by the Common Sense Model
item.description: Tracking adults with gastrointestinal conditions during the pandemic, this study found a reciprocal relationship between gastrointestinal symptoms and psychological distress.

item.title: Disease course and treatment outcomes of Crohn's disease patients with early or late surgery – A Danish nationwide cohort study from 1997 to 2015
item.description: A study testing surgery timing in Crohn's disease patients found that undergoing surgery within 29 days of diagnosis reduced the risks of repeat surgery and subsequent medication use.
